title: storage: preserve lower-layer metadata for implicit directories
body:
## Summary

Fix overlay layer application when a diff adds files under an existing directory but does not include an explicit entry for that directory itself.

In that case, the storage code may create the parent directory implicitly so extraction can proceed. Before this change, that implicit directory could be created with default metadata such as `0755` instead of preserving the metadata from the lower layer.

This is what led to visible symptoms like `/tmp` ending up with the wrong mode in Podman/Buildah-based workflows.

## Why this matters

Directory metadata is part of the filesystem state.

If a lower layer already defines a directory and an upper layer only adds children inside it, applying that upper layer must not silently redefine the directory's mode, ownership, timestamps, or xattrs.

Without that guarantee, image extraction can introduce permission regressions and runtime differences that are hard to trace back to the storage layer.

## What changed

This patch fixes both paths where implicit directories can appear:

- direct `overlay.ApplyDiff`
- staged/chunked `PrepareStagedLayer -> ApplyStagedLayer -> overlay.ApplyDiffFromStagingDirectory`

For the direct tar path:
- detect missing parent directories in the diff
- synthesize those directory entries
- backfill their metadata from the lower layers before extraction

For the staged/chunked path:
- track directories created implicitly during extraction into the staging area
- restore their lower-layer metadata before finalizing the layer

## Tests

Added regression coverage for both cases:

- `storage/tests/apply-diff.bats`
  - direct overlay apply with omitted parent directory entries
- `storage/staged_applydiff_linux_test.go`
  - exact staged runtime path through `PrepareStagedLayer` and `ApplyStagedLayer`

The staged integration test reproduces the real path and verifies that lower-layer directory modes are preserved instead of collapsing to default `0755`.

## Related issues

- Related to containers/buildah#6503
- Related to containers/podman#27131
- Related to devcontainers/features#1480
